content.problemStatement:
Your friend works as a valet where NNN cars show up in a shift. The iii-th car will arrive TiT_iTi​ seconds after the current moment, and the car can wait for at most YiY_iYi​ seconds. That is, your friend can only start parking the car from time TiT_iTi​ to Ti+YiT_i+Y_iTi​+Yi​ (inclusive).

Additionally, it takes XiX_iXi​ time to park the iii-th car, which means he cannot park any other cars for XiX_iXi​ seconds starting from the time he starts parking the iii-th car. It is known that no driver would like to wait for more time than it takes to park his car, so that Yi<XiY_i  < X_iYi​<Xi​ for all iii. The driver of the iii-th car will pay AiA_iAi​ coins as a tip to your friend if his car is parked. Note that he can start parking the next car immediately after he finishes parking the previous car.

Your friend has asked you what will be the maximize possible earnings if he optimally chooses which cars to park and at what times.

###Input:



The first line contains one single integer NNN (1≤N≤3⋅105)(1 \le N \le 3 \cdot 10^5)(1≤N≤3⋅105) −-− the number of cars.




The second line contains NNN integers T1,T2,…,TnT_1, T_2, \ldots, T_nT1​,T2​,…,Tn​ −-− the time (in seconds) at which iii-th car arrives.




The third line contains NNN integers X1,X2,…,XnX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_nX1​,X2​,…,Xn​ −-− the time (in seconds) required to park the iii-th car.




The fourth line contains NNN integers Y1,Y2,…,YnY_1, Y_2, \ldots, Y_nY1​,Y2​,…,Yn​ −-− the maximum time (in seconds) for which the iii-th car can wait.




The fifth line contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,AnA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_nA1​,A2​,…,An​ −-− the tip which the iii-th car driver gives after the car is parked.



###Output:
Print a single integer −-− the answer to the problem.

###Constraints



1≤N≤3⋅1051\le N\le 3\cdot 10^51≤N≤3⋅105




1≤Ti≤4⋅1031\le T_i\le 4\cdot 10^31≤Ti​≤4⋅103




0≤Yi<Xi≤4⋅1030\le Y_i < X_i\le 4\cdot 10^30≤Yi​<Xi​≤4⋅103




1≤Ai≤1091\le A_i\le 10^91≤Ai​≤109

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
It is optimal to start parking the first car at 555 seconds. We finish parking at 888 seconds, and immediately start parking the second car.
